questions.text: In the preparation of a soap, the fatty acid containing less than eight carbon atoms is not used, because the soap obtained:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Forms a colloidal solution with water
options.explanation: Soaps are not colloidal solutions, they are soluble in water. A colloidal solution is a type of mixture in which the dispersed particles are much smaller than 1 micrometer. Soap molecules are much larger than this, so they would not form a colloidal solution with water.

This is true, but it is not the reason why fatty acids with less than eight carbon atoms are not used in the preparation of soap. As I mentioned before, the main reason is that these acids have short hydrophobic tails that cannot form strong interactions with grease.

options.text: Does not form an emulsion with the grease
options.explanation: Soap has a tadpole-like structure because it possesses a long carboxylic acid chain through which hydrocarbons and sodium-potassium ions are attached. Ions dissolve in water and a non-ionic carbon chain dissolves in oil and forms an emulsion but to form an emulsion non-ionic chain must have more than 8 carbon atoms.

options.text: Forms an emulsion with the grease
options.explanation: This is true, but only if the fatty acids used to make the soap have long enough hydrophobic tails. Fatty acids with less than eight carbon atoms have short hydrophobic tails, so they cannot form strong interactions with grease.

questions.explanation: Soap has a tadpole like structure, because they possess a long carboxylic acid chain through which hydrocarbons and sodium-pottasium ions are attached. The ionic part dissolves in water whereas the non-ionic carbon chain dissolves in oil and forms an emulsion, but to form an emulsion, the non-ionic chain must have more than 8 carbon atoms.

questions.text: Which of the following is the main component of bathing soap?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Caustic potash is a strong base that is used to make soap, but it is not the main component.
Glycerine is a byproduct of soapmaking, and it is often added to soap to make it more moisturizing.
Potassium glycolate is a salt of glycolic acid, and it is sometimes added to soap to make it more exfoliating.
Bathing soap are Na+ or K+ salts of carboxylic acid. Potassium stearate is K+ salt of carboxylic acid (C17H35CO2K).

questions.explanation: Bathing soap are Na+ or K+ salts of carboxylic acid. Potassium stearate is K+ salt of carboxylic acid (C17H35CO2K).

questions.text: Which of the following catalysts is used in the modern method of preparation of soap by hydrolysis of fat using steam?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Not correct because catalyst ZnO is used in modern method of preparation of soap by hydrolysis of fat using steam.
Not correct because catalyst ZnO is used in modern method of preparation of soap by hydrolysis of fat using steam.
In the preparation of soap by hydrolysis of fat using steam in presence of catalyst ZnO, the ester linkage bond breaks into glycerol and soap.
Not correct because catalyst ZnO is used in modern method of preparation of soap by hydrolysis of fat using steam.

questions.explanation: In the preparation of soap by hydrolysis of fat using steam in presence of catalyst ZnO, the ester linkage bond breaks into glycerol and soap.

questions.text: Which of the following substaces is not used as a sedative medicine?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Due to the high solubility of Oxalyl urea, it is used as fertilizer.
Diazepam is used to treat anxiety by inducing sleep.
Veronal is the trade name for a barbiturate drug and is used to induce sleep by suppressing the brain.
Eszopiclone is used to treat anxiety by inducing sleep.

questions.explanation: Veronal is the trade name for a barbiturate drug and is used to induce sleep by suppressing the brain. Eszopiclone and diazepam are used to treat anxiety by inducing sleep. Due to the high solubility of Oxalyl urea, it is used as fertilizer.

questions.text: Benzidineis are:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Benzidine and its derivatives are not used as analgesics (painkillers).
Benzidine and its derivatives are not used as antiallergic medications.
Benzidineis are antidepressants that can help relieve symptoms of depression, anxiety disorders, social anxiety disorder etc.
While benzidine itself is not used for medicinal purposes due to its carcinogenic properties, it has been used historically in the production of dyes and is a known industrial chemical.

questions.explanation: Benzidineis are antidepressants that can help relieve symptoms of depression, anxiety disorders, social anxiety disorder etc.

questions.text: Which is not considered as inorganic antiseptic:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chlorine is commonly used as an inorganic antiseptic in the form of chlorine-based disinfectants.
Iodine is commonly used as an inorganic antiseptic in the form of iodine solutions or iodine-based antiseptics.
Sulfur dioxide has some antiseptic properties and is used in certain applications as a preservative and antimicrobial agent

Bromine is not typically considered an inorganic antiseptic. While bromine compounds can have antimicrobial properties, they are not commonly used as antiseptics compared to chlorine (Cl2), iodine (I2), and sulfur dioxide (SO2), which are more commonly used for their antiseptic properties.

questions.explanation: Commonly used inorganic antiseptics are Cl2, I2 and SO2

questions.text: Which of the following is a hypotonic drug?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Salol, catechol and phenol are used as antiseptic. Luminal is used to treat anxiety and sleep disorders so it works as hypotonic drug.

Salol is an organic compound that has been used historically as an analgesic (pain reliever) and antipyretic (fever reducer). It is not typically considered a hypotonic drug. Salol is known for its mild antiseptic properties and has been used in the past as an ingredient in topical ointments.

Catechol is a dihydroxybenzene compound that is used in organic synthesis and as a precursor to various pharmaceuticals. It is not considered a hypotonic drug. Catechol itself is not used for therapeutic purposes but is important in the production of chemicals and pharmaceuticals.

Phenol is an aromatic organic compound that is used in various industrial applications and as a disinfectant. It is not typically considered a hypotonic drug. Phenol has antiseptic properties and has been used in the past as a disinfectant for skin and surfaces. However, its use has decreased due to its potential toxicity and irritant properties

questions.explanation: Salol, catechol and phenol are used as antiseptic. Luminal is used to treat anxiety and sleep disorders so it works as hypotonic drug.

questions.text: Deepening of colour of compounds may be caused by which of the following:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Deepening of compounds colour is due to bathochromic groups, because in presence of these groups bathochromic shift (Shifting of different spectrum of molecules towards lower frequency) occurs.
Hypsochromic groups cause a shift in the absorption spectrum towards shorter wavelengths, resulting in a decrease in color intensity.They do not cause a deepening of color, as stated in the question.

Chromogens are substances responsible for color formation in compounds but do not necessarily cause a deepening of color on their own. While they contribute to the coloration of compounds, they are not the direct cause of a deepening of color as described in the question.
Option A is correct as it directly addresses the cause of deepening color.

questions.explanation: Deepening of compounds colour is due to bathochromic groups, because in presence of these groups bathochromic shift (Shifting of different spectrum of molecules towards lower frequency) occurs.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a part of green chemistry?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The study of chemical reactions, isomerizations and physical behavior that may occur under the influence of visible and/or ultraviolet light is called Photochemistry.

Sonochemistry is the research area in which molecules undergo a chemical reaction because of the application of powerful ultrasound radiation (20 kHz–10 MHz). The physical phenomenon responsible for the sonochemical process is acoustic cavitation.

Under the green chemistry such chemical products are designed that can reduce the use or production of hazardous substances. Nuclear chemistry deals with radioactivity, nuclear processes etc. that are not safe for our environment so it is not the part of green chemistry.

Biochemistry explores chemical processes related to living organisms. It is a laboratory-based science combining biology and chemistry. Biochemists study the structure, composition, and chemical reactions of substances in living systems and, in turn, their functions and ways to control them.

questions.explanation: Under the green chemistry such chemical products are designed that can reduce the use or production of hazardous substances. Nuclear chemistry deals with radioactivity, nuclear processes etc. that are not safe for our environment so it is not the part of green chemistry.

questions.text: Hydrolysis of an oil by caustic alkali is called?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is a reaction which involves formation of an ester. Hence, this is not the correct answer.

The alkaline hydrolysis of oils and fats is called Saponification. The ester, on treating with a base such as NaOH is converted back to alcohol and sodium salt of carboxylic acid. The reaction is known as saponification because it is used in the manufacture of soap. Hence, this is the correct answer.

Acetylation is a chemical reaction that involves the addition of an acetyl group to a molecule. This process is commonly used to modify the properties of organic molecules, such as making them more soluble in water or more resistant to degradation. Hence, this is not the correct answer.
Carboxylation is a chemical reaction that involves the addition of a carboxyl group to a molecule. Hence, this is not the correct answer.

questions.explanation: The alkaline hydrolysis of oils and fats is called Saponification. The ester, on treating with a base such as NaOH is converted back to alcohol and sodium salt of carboxylic acid. The reaction is known as saponification because it is used in the manufacture of soap.

questions.text: Which of the following compounds is formed on catalytic hydrogenolysis of triolein?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydrogenolysis is a chemical reaction that involves the cleavage or breaking of chemical bonds in a molecule using hydrogen gas (H2) in the presence of a suitable catalyst.Catalysts commonly used in hydrogenolysis reactions include metals such as platinum (Pt), palladium (Pd), nickel (Ni), and other transition metals.When hydrogen adds to all of the double bonds of glyceryl trioleate (triolein) using a nickel catalyst, the product is the saturated fat glyceryl tristearate (tristearin). Hence, this is not the correct option.

Hydrogenolysis is a chemical reaction that involves the cleavage or breaking of chemical bonds in a molecule using hydrogen gas (H2) in the presence of a suitable catalyst.Catalysts commonly used in hydrogenolysis reactions include metals such as platinum (Pt), palladium (Pd), nickel (Ni), and other transition metals.When hydrogen adds to all of the double bonds of glyceryl trioleate (triolein) using a nickel catalyst, the product is the saturated fat glyceryl tristearate (tristearin). Hence, this is not the correct option.

Hydrogenolysis is a chemical reaction that involves the cleavage or breaking of chemical bonds in a molecule using hydrogen gas (H2) in the presence of a suitable catalyst.Catalysts commonly used in hydrogenolysis reactions include metals such as platinum (Pt), palladium (Pd), nickel (Ni), and other transition metals.When hydrogen adds to all of the double bonds of glyceryl trioleate (triolein) using a nickel catalyst, the product is the saturated fat glyceryl tristearate (tristearin). Hence, this is not the correct option.

Hydrogenolysis is a chemical reaction that involves the cleavage or breaking of chemical bonds in a molecule using hydrogen gas (H2) in the presence of a suitable catalyst.Catalysts commonly used in hydrogenolysis reactions include metals such as platinum (Pt), palladium (Pd), nickel (Ni), and other transition metals.When hydrogen adds to all of the double bonds of glyceryl trioleate (triolein) using a nickel catalyst, the product is the saturated fat glyceryl tristearate (tristearin). Hence, this is the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Hydrogenolysis is a chemical reaction that involves the cleavage or breaking of chemical bonds in a molecule using hydrogen gas (H2) in the presence of a suitable catalyst.Catalysts commonly used in hydrogenolysis reactions include metals such as platinum (Pt), palladium (Pd), nickel (Ni), and other transition metals.When hydrogen adds to all of the double bonds of glyceryl trioleate (triolein) using a nickel catalyst, the product is the saturated fat glyceryl tristearate (tristearin).

questions.text: Which of the following compounds is an example of an non–ionic detergent?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This compound belongs to the class of quaternary ammonium compounds and is an example of a cationic detergent. Cationic detergents are positively charged and are often used in fabric softeners and certain cleaning agents. Hence, this is not the correct option.

This compound is an anionic detergent. Anionic detergents have a negatively charged hydrophilic group and are often used in household cleaning products and personal care items such as shampoos and soaps. Hence, this is not the correct option.

Pentaerythritolmonostearate(C23H46O5) is an example of a non-ionic detergent. Non-ionic detergents do not carry a net charge and are commonly used in laundry detergents, as they are gentle on fabrics and are less likely to cause skin irritation.

It is anionic surfactantThis compound is an anionic detergent. Like other anionic detergents, it has a negatively charged hydrophilic group and is used in various cleaning applications. Hence, this is not the correct option.

questions.explanation: Pentaerythritolmonostearate(C23H46O5) is an example of a non-ionic detergent. Non-ionic detergents do not carry a net charge and are commonly used in laundry detergents, as they are gentle on fabrics and are less likely to cause skin irritation.

questions.text: The substance which affects the Central Nervous System and induces sleep are called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: These are substances that reduce fever by acting on the hypothalamus, resetting the body's temperature set-point. Antipyretics like paracetamol (acetaminophen) or ibuprofen do not primarily induce sleep; their primary function is to lower fever. Hence, this is not the correct option.
Tranquilizers, also known as sedatives or hypnotics, are drugs that act on the CNS to reduce anxiety, promote relaxation, and induce sleep. Hence, this is the correct answer.
Analgesics are pain-relieving medications that work by blocking pain signals to the brain. Examples include aspirin, ibuprofen, and opioids like morphine. Hence, this is not the correct option.

Tranquilizers, also known as sedatives or hypnotics, are drugs that act on the CNS to reduce anxiety, promote relaxation, and induce sleep. Hence, this is the correct answer. Thus, option b is correct.

questions.explanation: Tranquilizers, also known as sedatives or hypnotics, are drugs that act on the CNS to reduce anxiety, promote relaxation, and induce sleep.

questions.text: Primary structure of proteins shows:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The three dimensional twisting and folding of the polypeptide chain results in the tertiary structure of proteins not primary structure. Hence, this is not the correct option.

The primary structure of a protein refers to the linear sequence of amino acids that make up the protein chain. It is the most fundamental level of protein structure and is determined by the specific arrangement of amino acids in the polypeptide chain. Hence, this is the correct answer.

The secondary structure of a protein is a regular coiling or zigzagging of polypeptide chains caused by hydrogen bonding between NH and C = 0 groups of amino acids near each other in the chains. Therefore, coiling is not responsible for primary structure and this is not the correct option.

options.text: It is due to hydrogen bonding
options.explanation: Hydrogen bonding is prominently responsible for secondary structure not primary. Hence, this is not the correct option.

questions.explanation: The primary structure of a protein refers to the linear sequence of amino acids that make up the protein chain. It is the most fundamental level of protein structure and is determined by the specific arrangement of amino acids in the polypeptide chain.

questions.text: Primary structure of protein arises due to which force?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydrogen bonding plays a crucial role in stabilizing secondary and tertiary structures of proteins. In secondary structures like alpha helices and beta sheets, hydrogen bonds form between the backbone atoms of the amino acids. These bonds help to maintain the specific folding patterns of the protein.
Covalent bond is correct for the primary structure of proteins, as it refers to the bonds formed between the amino acids in the sequence, creating the peptide backbone of the protein.

Debye forces are weak intermolecular forces that arise due to temporary fluctuations in electron distribution around atoms and molecules. These forces contribute to the overall stability of protein structures by facilitating interactions between non-polar amino acid side chains, particularly in the hydrophobic core of the protein.

Dipole-dipole forces occur between polar molecules or polar regions within molecules. In proteins, these forces can arise between polar amino acid side chains or between polar and charged groups. They contribute to the specificity and stability of protein-protein interactions in the tertiary and quaternary structures of proteins.

questions.explanation: Covalent bond is correct for the primary structure of proteins, as it refers to the bonds formed between the amino acids in the sequence, creating the peptide backbone of the protein.

questions.text: What common name can be given to lipids, proteins, carbohydrates and nucleic acid polymers?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: While lipids, proteins, carbohydrates, and nucleic acids are indeed organic polymers, this term is too broad and encompasses a wider range of polymers that may not necessarily be produced by living organisms.

This term refers to polymers that do not contain carbon-hydrogen bonds and are not produced by living organisms. Lipids, proteins, carbohydrates, and nucleic acids are all organic and therefore not classified as inorganic polymers.

Biopolymers are a class of polymers that are produced by living organisms and are made up of repeating units of monomers. Lipids, proteins, carbohydrates, and nucleic acids are all examples of biopolymers because they are composed of organic molecules and are essential components of living organisms.

This term refers to polymers that are artificially produced by humans through chemical synthesis. Lipids, proteins, carbohydrates, and nucleic acids are naturally occurring biopolymers and are not synthetic.

questions.explanation: Most biologically important substances are organic compounds (Hiopolymers) built up from skeleton of carbon atoms. Many of them are very large mo1ecq1cand most of these are polymers. The four major classes of organic compounds on living cells are carbohydrates, lipids, proteins and nucleic acids.

questions.text: The substrate binds with the enzyme at:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option suggests a site on the enzyme that is polar in nature, meaning it has regions of partial positive and negative charge. However, substrate binding typically occurs at specific active sites on the enzyme, which may or may not be polar.

This option implies a site on the enzyme that is positively charged. While some enzymes may have regions with a net positive charge, substrate binding generally occurs at the active site, which may not necessarily be cationic.

This is the correct option. The active site is the specific region on the enzyme where the substrate binds and undergoes the catalytic reaction. It has a complementary shape and chemical environment to the substrate.

This option suggests a site on the enzyme that is negatively charged. Similar to the previous options, while some regions of enzymes may have a net negative charge, substrate binding typically occurs at the active site, which may not necessarily be anionic.

questions.explanation: This is the correct option. The active site is the specific region on the enzyme where the substrate binds and undergoes the catalytic reaction. It has a complementary shape and chemical environment to the substrate.

questions.text: The activator for chrome oxidase is?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The activator for cytochrome oxidase is Fe2+. Cytochrome oxidase is an enzyme that is found in the electron transport chain of mitochondria. It is the final enzyme in the electron transport chain, and it catalyzes the reduction of oxygen to water. The cofactor for cytochrome oxidase is heme a3, which is a metal-containing molecule. The iron atom in heme a3 is in the Fe2+ state. This iron atom is the site of oxygen reduction. The Fe2+ state is the most active state of iron for oxygen reduction. When Fe2+ is oxidized to Fe3+, it is less active for oxygen reduction. The Fe2+ cofactor is essential for the activity of cytochrome oxidase. Without the Fe2+ cofactor, cytochrome oxidase would not be able to reduce oxygen to water.

Zn2+ is a cofactor for carbonic anhydrase. Carbonic anhydrase is an enzyme that catalyzes the following reaction: CO2 + H2O <=> H2CO3 <=> HCO3- + H+ . This reaction is the first step in the process of respiration. Carbonic anhydrase is found in red blood cells, where it helps to regulate the pH of the blood. The zinc atom in carbonic anhydrase is essential for its activity. The zinc atom binds to the carbon dioxide molecule and helps to facilitate the reaction. Carbonic anhydrase is a very efficient enzyme. It can catalyze the reaction between carbon dioxide and water millions of times per second.

Mg2+ is a cofactor for glucose-6-phosphatase. Glucose-6-phosphatase is an enzyme that catalyzes the following reaction:Glucose-6-phosphate <=> glucose + phosphate.This reaction is the final step in the breakdown of glucose in the liver and kidneys. Glucose-6-phosphatase is responsible for releasing glucose into the bloodstream. The magnesium atom in glucose-6-phosphatase is essential for its activity. The magnesium atom binds to the phosphate group on the glucose-6-phosphate molecule and helps to facilitate the reaction.

The activator for cytochrome oxidase is Fe2+. Cytochrome oxidase is an enzyme that is found in the electron transport chain of mitochondria. It is the final enzyme in the electron transport chain, and it catalyzes the reduction of oxygen to water. The cofactor for cytochrome oxidase is heme a3, which is a metal-containing molecule. The iron atom in heme a3 is in the Fe2+ state. This iron atom is the site of oxygen reduction. The Fe2+ state is the most active state of iron for oxygen reduction. When Fe2+ is oxidized to Fe3+, it is less active for oxygen reduction. The Fe2+ cofactor is essential for the activity of cytochrome oxidase. Without the Fe2+ cofactor, cytochrome oxidase would not be able to reduce oxygen to water. Thus, option A is correct.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct.The activator for cytochrome oxidase is Fe2+. Cytochrome oxidase is an enzyme that is found in the electron transport chain of mitochondria. It is the final enzyme in the electron transport chain, and it catalyzes the reduction of oxygen to water. The cofactor for cytochrome oxidase is heme a3, which is a metal-containing molecule. The iron atom in heme a3 is in the Fe2+ state. This iron atom is the site of oxygen reduction. The Fe2+ state is the most active state of iron for oxygen reduction. When Fe2+ is oxidized to Fe3+, it is less active for oxygen reduction. The Fe2+ cofactor is essential for the activity of cytochrome oxidase. Without the Fe2+ cofactor, cytochrome oxidase would not be able to reduce oxygen to water.

questions.text: Which of the following acids cannot be prepared directly from carboxylic acid?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Acid halide can be prepared directly from a carboxylic acid. It requires the use of a reagent, such as thionyl chloride. For example, acetic acid can be reacted with thionyl chloride to form acetyl chloride. CH3COOH + SOCl2 <=> CH3COCl + SO2 + HCl

Acid amide cannot be prepared by the reaction of a carboxylic acid with ammonia. Carboxylic acids react with ammonia to form ammonium salts which on heating produce acid amides. CH3COOH + NH3 --> CH3COONH4 + H2O CH3COONH4 --> CH3CONH2
Ester can be prepared by the reaction of a carboxylic acid with an alcohol. For example, acetic acid and methanol can be reacted to form methyl acetate. CH3COOH + CH3OH <=> CH3COOCH3 + H2O

Acid anhydride can be prepared by the dehydration of two carboxylic acids. For example, acetic acid and propanoic acid can be dehydrated to form propanoic anhydride. CH3COOH + CH3CH2COOH <=> (CH3CO)2O + H2O

questions.explanation: Acid amide cannot be prepared by the reaction of a carboxylic acid with ammonia. Carboxylic acids react with ammonia to form ammonium salts which on heating produce acid amides. CH3COOH + NH3 --> CH3COONH4 + H2O CH3COONH4 --> CH3CONH2

questions.text: Which one of the following organic acids is the most reactive and the strongest acid?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Formic acid is the simplest carboxylic acid. It has a pKa value of 3.75, which is the lowest among the given acids. The pKa value is a measure of the acidity of a molecule. A lower pKa value means that the molecule is more acidic. The other acids have the following pKa values: CH3COOH: pKa = 4.76 CH3CH2COOH: pKa = 5.07 CH3CH2CH2COOH: pKa = 4.89 As you can see, the pKa value of formic acid is lower than the pKa values of the other acids. This means that formic acid is more acidic than the other acids. Formic acid is more reactive than the other acids because it has a smaller size and a more electronegative carbon atom. The smaller size of formic acid makes it more likely to react with other molecules. The more electronegative carbon atom of formic acid makes it more likely to donate a proton to other molecules.
Acetic acid is a weak acid compared to the other options. It partially dissociates in water, releasing H+ ions, but it's not as reactive or strong as some other organic acids.
Propionic acid is also a weak acid, similar to acetic acid. It's not as reactive or strong as stronger organic acids.
Butyric acid is also a weak acid. It's longer in chain length compared to acetic and propionic acid, but still not considered highly reactive or strong in terms of acidity..

questions.explanation: Option A is correct.Formic acid is the simplest carboxylic acid. It has a pKa value of 3.75, which is the lowest among the given acids. The pKa value is a measure of the acidity of a molecule. A lower pKa value means that the molecule is more acidic. The other acids have the following pKa values: CH3COOH: pKa = 4.76 CH3CH2COOH: pKa = 5.07 CH3CH2CH2COOH: pKa = 4.89 As you can see, the pKa value of formic acid is lower than the pKa values of the other acids. This means that formic acid is more acidic than the other acids. Formic acid is more reactive than the other acids because it has a smaller size and a more electronegative carbon atom. The smaller size of formic acid makes it more likely to react with other molecules. The more electronegative carbon atom of formic acid makes it more likely to donate a proton to other molecules.
